sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ξιφήρης πρὸς δόμοις λοχᾷς ἐμοῖς ;
sections.translation: Then why are you in ambush , with drawn sword , near my house ?

sections.label: Section 226
sections.original: μείνασʼ ἄκουσον , καὶ τάχʼ οὐκ ἄλλως ἐρεῖς .
sections.translation: Wait and hear , and you will soon say the same .

sections.label: Section 227
sections.original: ἕστηκα · πάντως δʼ εἰμὶ σή · κρείσσων γὰρ εἶ .
sections.translation: I am still ; in any case , I am yours , since you are the stronger .

sections.label: Section 228
sections.original: ἥκω φέρων σοι σοῦ κασιγνήτου λόγους .
sections.translation: I have come to bring you word from your brother .

sections.label: Section 229
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , ἆρα ζῶντος ἢ τεθνηκότος ;
sections.translation: Oh best of friends ! Is he alive or dead ?

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: ζῇ · πρῶτα γάρ σοι τἀγάθʼ ἀγγέλλειν θέλω .
sections.translation: Alive ; for I want to tell you the good news first .

sections.label: Section 231
sections.original: εὐδαιμονοίης , μισθὸν ἡδίστων λόγων .
sections.translation: May you be happy , as a reward for your most welcome tidings .

sections.label: Section 232
sections.original: κοινῇ δίδωμι τοῦτο νῷν ἀμφοῖν ἔχειν .
sections.translation: I give that blessing for us two to have in common .

sections.label: Section 233
sections.original: ποῦ γῆς ὁ τλήμων τλήμονας φυγὰς ἔχων ;
sections.translation: Where does the unhappy one live , in his unhappy exile ?

sections.label: Section 234
sections.original: οὐχ ἕνα νομίζων φθείρεται πόλεως νόμον .
sections.translation: He goes miserably about , not following the customs of any one city .

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: οὔ που σπανίζων τοῦ καθʼ ἡμέραν βίου ;
sections.translation: Surely he does not want for a living , day by day ?

sections.label: Section 236
sections.original: ἔχει μέν , ἀσθενὴς δὲ δὴ φεύγων ἀνήρ .
sections.translation: He has that , but an exile is a helpless man at best .

sections.label: Section 237
sections.original: λόγον δὲ δὴ τίνʼ ἦλθες ἐκ κείνου φέρων ;
sections.translation: What is this message you have come bringing from him ?

sections.label: Section 238
sections.original: εἰ ζῇς , ὅπως τε ζῶσα συμφορᾶς ἔχεις .
sections.translation: If you are alive , and if so , how you are .

sections.label: Section 239
sections.original: οὐκοῦν ὁρᾷς μου πρῶτον ὡς ξηρὸν δέμας .
sections.translation: Well then , you see first of all how withered my body is .

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: λύπαις γε συντετηκός , ὥστε με στένειν .
sections.translation: Yes , so wasted with sorrow that I sigh for it .

sections.label: Section 241
sections.original: καὶ κρᾶτα πλόκαμόν τʼ ἐσκυθισμένον ξυρῷ .
sections.translation: And my head and hair , close shaven as if by a Scythian ’ s razor .

sections.label: Section 242
sections.original: δάκνει σʼ ἀδελφὸς ὅ τε θανὼν ἴσως πατήρ .
sections.translation: Your brother and your dead father perhaps gnaw at your heart .

sections.label: Section 243
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί γάρ μοι τῶνδέ γʼ ἐστὶ φίλτερον ;
sections.translation: Alas ! For what is dearer to me than they ?

sections.label: Section 244
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · τί δαὶ σὺ σῷ κασιγνήτῳ , δοκεῖς ;
sections.translation: Ah ! What are you to your brother , do you think ?

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: ἀπὼν ἐκεῖνος , οὐ παρὼν ἡμῖν φίλος .
sections.translation: He is far away , not here to be my friend .

sections.label: Section 246
sections.original: ἐκ τοῦ δὲ ναίεις ἐνθάδʼ ἄστεως ἑκάς ;
sections.translation: Why are you living here , far from the city ?

sections.label: Section 247
sections.original: ἐγημάμεσθʼ , ὦ ξεῖνε , θανάσιμον γάμον .
sections.translation: I am married , stranger ; a deadly match .

sections.label: Section 248
sections.original: ᾤμωξʼ ἀδελφὸν σόν . Μυκηναίων τίνι ;
sections.translation: I pity your brother . Is your husband a Mycenaean ?

sections.label: Section 249
sections.original: οὐχ ᾧ πατήρ μʼ ἤλπιζεν ἐκδώσειν ποτέ .
sections.translation: Not one to whom my father ever hoped to give me .

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: εἴφʼ , ὡς ἀκούσας σῷ κασιγνήτῳ λέγω .
sections.translation: Tell me so that I may hear and inform your brother .

sections.label: Section 251
sections.original: ἐν τοῖσδʼ ἐκείνου τηλορὸς ναίω δόμοις .
sections.translation: I live in his house , at a distance from the city .

sections.label: Section 252
sections.original: σκαφεύς τις ἢ βουφορβὸς ἄξιος δόμων .
sections.translation: A ditch-digger or a herdsman is worthy of the house .

sections.label: Section 253
sections.original: πένης ἀνὴρ γενναῖος ἔς τʼ ἔμʼ εὐσεβής .
sections.translation: He is a man poor but noble , and respectful to me .

sections.label: Section 254
sections.original: ἡ δʼ εὐσέβεια τίς πρόσεστι σῷ πόσει ;
sections.translation: What is this respect that your husband has ?

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: οὐπώποτʼ εὐνῆς τῆς ἐμῆς ἔτλη θιγεῖν .
sections.translation: He has never dared to touch me in bed .

sections.label: Section 256
sections.original: ἅγνευμʼ ἔχων τι θεῖον ἤ σʼ ἀπαξιῶν ;
sections.translation: Does he hold some form of religious chastity , or does he think you unworthy ?

sections.label: Section 257
sections.original: γονέας ὑβρίζειν τοὺς ἐμοὺς οὐκ ἠξίου .
sections.translation: He did not think himself worthy to insult my family .

sections.label: Section 258
sections.original: καὶ πῶς γάμον τοιοῦτον οὐχ ἥσθη λαβών ;
sections.translation: And how was he not delighted to make such a marriage ?

sections.label: Section 259
sections.original: οὐ κύριον τὸν δόντα μʼ ἡγεῖται , ξένε .
sections.translation: He thinks the person who gave me did not have that right , stranger .

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: ξυνῆκʼ · Ὀρέστῃ μή ποτʼ ἐκτείσῃ δίκην .
sections.translation: I understand ; he fears that he may someday be punished by Orestes .

sections.label: Section 261
sections.original: τοῦτʼ αὐτὸ ταρβῶν , πρὸς δὲ καὶ σώφρων ἔφυ .
sections.translation: He does fear that , but he is also a virtuous man .

sections.label: Section 262
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! You have described a noble man , and he must be treated well .

sections.label: Section 263
sections.original: εἰ δή ποθʼ ἥξει γʼ ἐς δόμους ὁ νῦν ἀπών .
sections.translation: Yes , if ever the one now absent comes home .

sections.label: Section 264
sections.original: μήτηρ δέ σʼ ἡ τεκοῦσα ταῦτʼ ἠνέσχετο ;
sections.translation: But did your mother , who gave you birth , endure this ?

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: γυναῖκες ἀνδρῶν , ὦ ξένʼ , οὐ παίδων φίλαι .
sections.translation: Women love their husbands , stranger , not their children .

sections.label: Section 266
sections.original: τίνος δέ σʼ οὕνεχʼ ὕβρισʼ Αἴγισθος τάδε ;
sections.translation: Why did Aegisthus insult you in this way ?

sections.label: Section 267
sections.original: τεκεῖν μʼ ἐβούλετʼ ἀσθενῆ , τοιῷδε δούς .
sections.translation: He planned for me to bear powerless children , when he gave me to such a man .

sections.label: Section 268
sections.original: ὡς δῆθε παῖδας μὴ τέκοις ποινάτορας ;
sections.translation: So that you might not bear sons to punish him , of course ?

sections.label: Section 269
sections.original: τοιαῦτʼ ἐβούλευσʼ · ὧν ἐμοὶ δοίη δίκην .
sections.translation: That was his plan ; may he make amends to me for it !

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: οἶδεν δέ σʼ οὖσαν παρθένον μητρὸς πόσις ;
sections.translation: Does your mother ’ s husband know that you are a virgin ?

sections.label: Section 271
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδε · σιγῇ τοῦθʼ ὑφαιρούμεσθά νιν .
sections.translation: He does not know ; we steal that from him by our silence .

sections.label: Section 272
sections.original: αἵδʼ οὖν φίλαι σοι τούσδʼ ἀκούουσιν λόγους ;
sections.translation: Are these women who hear our talk friends of yours ?

sections.label: Section 273
sections.original: ὥστε στέγειν γε τἀμὰ καὶ σʼ ἔπη καλῶς .
sections.translation: They will keep both your words and mine well hidden .

sections.label: Section 274
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ Ὀρέστης πρὸς τόδʼ , Ἄργος ἢν μόλῃ ;
sections.translation: What then could Orestes do in this matter if he comes to Argos ?

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: ἤρου τόδʼ ; αἰσχρόν γʼ εἶπας · οὐ γὰρ νῦν ἀκμή ;
sections.translation: Do you ask this ? You have said a shameful word ; isn ’ t it the critical time now ?

sections.label: Section 276
sections.original: ἐλθὼν δὲ δὴ πῶς φονέας ἂν κτάνοι πατρός ;
sections.translation: But if he does come , how might he kill his father ’ s murderers ?

sections.label: Section 277
sections.original: τολμῶν ὑπʼ ἐχθρῶν οἷʼ ἐτολμήθη πατήρ .
sections.translation: By daring such things as his enemies dared against my father .

sections.label: Section 278
sections.original: ἦ καὶ μετʼ αὐτοῦ μητέρʼ ἂν τλαίης κτανεῖν ;
sections.translation: And would you dare , with him , to kill your mother ?

sections.label: Section 279
sections.original: ταὐτῷ γε πελέκει τῷ πατὴρ ἀπώλετο .
sections.translation: Yes , with that same axe by which my father died .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: λέγω τάδʼ αὐτῷ , καὶ βέβαια τἀπὸ σοῦ ;
sections.translation: Am I to tell him this , and that your purpose is steadfast ?

sections.label: Section 281
sections.original: θάνοιμι μητρὸς αἷμʼ ἐπισφάξασʼ ἐμῆς .
sections.translation: Once I had shed my mother ’ s blood , I might die !

sections.label: Section 282
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! Would that Orestes were near by , to hear that !

sections.label: Section 283
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ ξένʼ , οὐ γνοίην ἂν εἰσιδοῦσά νιν .
sections.translation: But , stranger , I would not know him if I saw him .

sections.label: Section 284
sections.original: νέα γάρ , οὐδὲν θαῦμʼ , ἀπεζεύχθης νέου .
sections.translation: No wonder , for you were both young when you were parted .
